role: A registry-driven pill switcher that lets a user pick one binding from a server-supplied list.
capabilities: Render a pill for each binding in the bindings prop
Notify caller via onPick when a pill is clicked
Apply a compact layout variant via CSS class
Stamp stable UI addresses on the container and each pill
substance_why: The core switching UI is implemented, but it lacks accessibility, keyboard control, empty-state handling, and any non-mouse interaction.
interactions_present: Mouse click on a pill invokes onPick(id)
Native title tooltip on hover via the title attribute
interactions_missing: Keyboard navigation and selection between pills
Focus management for the active pill
Confirmation or prevention of accidental switches
performance_risks: No memoization of pill buttons, so the full list re-renders on every prop change
encodeURIComponent and stamp calls run for every binding on every render
No virtualization, so very large binding lists could bloat the DOM
reuse_why: The registry-true, prop-driven design is worth keeping, but the component needs accessibility, keyboard handling, and styling modernization before reuse.
decomposition_hints: Extract a reusable Pill component for individual items
Extract a keyboard-navigation hook for left/right/home/end handling
Separate the address-stamping wrapper from the rendered control
